whatThisIs: A side-by-side staging reference for the spectrum of exertional and classic heat illness: heat cramps, heat exhaustion, and heat stroke. Sophie Well lays out the bedside discriminators that separate the stages - core temperature (heat exhaustion typically <40 C / 104 F with normal mentation, heat stroke >=40 C with altered mentation), skin findings, sweating presence, and the cardinal feature of heat stroke (CNS dysfunction - confusion, ataxia, seizure, coma - in the right environmental context). The tile is a pocket reference, not a calculator.
whenToUse: Use this in the field, the ED triage area, or at a mass-participation event (marathon, military training, outdoor labor) when a patient presents with hyperthermia and you need to decide whether to treat as heat exhaustion (oral or IV rehydration, removal from heat, passive cooling, observation) or as heat stroke (immediate aggressive active cooling - cold-water immersion is the first-line modality - and rapid transport). The single decision that drives mortality is recognizing CNS dysfunction in a hyperthermic patient and starting cooling within minutes rather than waiting for the rectal temperature to be charted. Pair the staging with the patient's medication list (anticholinergics, antipsychotics, stimulants) and underlying conditions.
